title: How Do You Manage Oedema After Tear Trough Filler？

segments.text: hi guys so and after the little chat about cannulas and needles on tear trough have triggered a really good question which is relevant no matter which instrument you use which is what do you do about a dima that occurs underneath the eye after having a tear trough procedure so firstly I would say about this is that we should kind of and think about the context which is that sometimes it can happen immediately and sometimes it can happen literally two years after the procedure and your management is different in those two cases so think about how long it's been since the procedure happened in terms of diagnosing what to do because it's really common if you've just had the procedure done that even up to a week afterwards you might have puffiness that's about to settle and it just doesn't and as quickly as in most cases so for many people there's nothing you do the procedure they get exactly what they want and everyone's happy and off they go but some people that get it gets

puffy after the procedure and it can be that way for up to a week so I wouldn't rush into being heavy handed right beginning with kind of reversing and massaging just let things settle as particularly truth has a bruised because bruising always causes swelling and so lumpiness after a filler treatment where there's been a bruise in that area is quite often just a recovery process it's basically in duration and it's a healing process that can cause a little bit of luck Vanessa anywhere on the face that's really noticeable and teardrop the reason this happens with teardrop is because you're so superficial so you're you it's the thinnest skin in the body that way that light and that means that the smallest little bit of imperfection is really easy to see so that's why it happens point to this is also useful to explain to patients so they know what's happened so it might be point two four millimeter thick here and just in your cheek it gets to about two millimeters so there's a really big difference there and it means you can't escape from little imperfection so a little bit of swelling a little bit of bruising they would have puffiness after

the procedure basically really normal and the problem is when it persists and you need to be able to give an explanation to your patients about persist and you need to give them a root out of that problem that gives them some certainty because it's quite upsetting let's be honest you have a procedure to try and make yourself look better and instead you get puffiness and you look Tyler and all I keep it up all night you know it's not great so they need some certainty so a root out of that even if you don't go rushing into higher layers straight away you need to at least paint the picture which is what I'm going to do for you today about how I would manage Perry office with d-mat warp or tear trough puffiness after a procedure so first thing is as I said explain it what's causing it can be swelling can be inflammation can be allergic reaction that's usually much more obvious like the reaction and it's involves erythema that seems to persist and most commonly it's just basically a little bit of water attraction that seems to make the filler that's because the filler pulls a bit of

moisture all because the filler reduces flow of fluid outside of the area these are the two theories it's really hard to tell which one's actually happening but if you consider that there are various lymph nodes in your tissue and the lymphatic drainage happens through the cheek and you put a filler on top of that area it might just slow down how quickly that fluid can leave us one of the theories you also hear mentioned so what do we do about it first thing is let's be practical let's assume by the way that this is none of the complications like reaction or infection or bruising this is a settle case there's nothing else going on but there's still some puffiness what do we do about it step one tell the patient a plan which is there they're basically a series of options you can massage it that will solve on the day maybe up to half of cases I can sometimes solve fairly quickly with a massage those are the my older ones step two is that they can massage themselves when they go home so they could do exactly that what you did for them and what you could they could do

that on themselves but you should show them how to and I'm going to show you how to do that on a model it is literally a model by the way it's not a real person and so and then stage three is you can highlight it so what's the route that you go on obviously a lot of people don't like going straight into hay lazing because it's you lose what you've done there may even be quite happy with it might just be a little bit puffy and but they kind of can really see the benefit and they don't want to go back to 0 and then try again so those are the options I say half of them you can solve on the day half of them half of what's left you might be able to solve with them massaging at home and then the others you need to hire Lee as unfortunately and start again so here's my little technique that I do with high lazing so I'm just gonna see if I can put this tripod down and then you can see Debbie there she is Debbie so Debbie had a tear trough treated now it's a little bit of arnica

gel so it doesn't have to be Annika gel it's anything slippery is absolutely fine procedure she notices and it could be two weeks after the procedure basically the puffiness starts and it's usually worse in the morning and then it persists all day long and there's no tenderness and there's no erythema and there's nothing else that you can think of that might be causing and she doesn't have a sinus infection she doesn't have a dental you know all the other things that might cause information in that area so all we have essentially the only thing that we have with debbie is a little bit of puffiness and a lower lid and nothing else and this is also by the way part of your examination because when you actually when you're actually doing this you're gonna get feedback from the process of massaging and that will tell you if there's a little bit of information because as soon as you start massaging the patient goes all that's really tender that's an indication that there's information and it might be worth waiting a little bit longer a tsunami massage it but just

don't go into high lazing if they're still tenderness because they might basically be some inflammation that will settle in a week or twos time and by the way this models name really is Debbie was the guy who makes these models his mom was called Debbie and that is actually her face so we all call her Debbie and it's not a name we've come up the building we do it widget use have a Deb you work with us and it caused us a lot of confusion when we were looking for Debbie because Debbie gets moved around by everyone so anyway this is the fake Debbie okay let's have a look at what I would do in terms of massage technique I hope you can see this well so a little bit of arnica gel each site and I put that arnica on my thumbs and particulate so it's nice and slippery and then I'm basically with the patient's eyes closed you're massaging down and out and this is following the lymphatic drainage so the lymph nodes basically run down here and drain into the cheek because we've got this thick teeth cheek tissue which is twenty to ten times thicker than the eyelid tissue

I should be able to hide some of this fluid now it's hard to get a sense of how hard I'm pushing but this is firm and slow and you're kind of it's basically a gel was woven in with the other tissue so it's quite important to be quite firm and slow to give it a chance to kind of squeeze out and it's gonna squeeze some of the product out but it won't take all of it out and you basically do this for three to five minutes and you're looking at the area as you do it and quite often it's quite a an instant and obvious response that you're down into the cheek and of course I'm also explaining at this time this is what you can do at home you can use some moisturizer anything that's slippery basically and just try and squeeze it down and out into your cheek when I am squeezing really quite firmly this is not a gentle massage this is not relaxing they often feel a little bit uncomfortable but it's making good progress and I would then

stop all the way out laughter oh I'll stop and have a look now you'll get an instant response from that as well because you're squeezing fluid out and you're hoping that you take in some fluid some fill out and then it's it might rebound afterwards as I said half then we're going to rebound they should then repeat that at home make an appointment at this time to see them again so I get the back in and maybe two weeks time and that time you'd be planning to reverse it if he's still making them unhappy so and reversing is pretty easy you often get a result within an hour or two this massage basically is also much better to do in the early stages of the problem so they've just had a treatment two weeks later they got puffiness you've got a chance of doing it in one go if it's been puffy or it's like that for 3-4 months after the procedure there's a bit less chance you be able to do with one massage it might take a bit longer and then if it's happened two years afterwards basically the filler breaking down causing demon you might still make a difference but for those ones I'm a bit more certain that they going to need

high layers and will make a difference quicker so that's about it when you're gonna high lays it if it's an old product and are a few people around like this I've seen someone have their th I've treated for five years before they came back with puffiness and that worked instantly for a little bit of high layers in there just disappeared it was really active almost quite frightening that she had quite big eye bags and they just went really quickly so you might get that when the filler is older I think it's kind of part of the breakdown process of all the fillers that they attract a bit more moisture so anyway I hope that helps thank you for watching just notice debbie you've been watching all along and I've been talking about Debbie that's what made me think that clarifying why we've called here debbie is a real debbie and hi it's okay come say hello if you want to be in life just doctor our minutes just come in and just talk about running a training okay um yeah they're running into cheeks and lips today so we're starting a new
